data.title: Britain's Royal Mail faces challenge of proving mail delivery; late letters causing distress, 500-year-old service criticized.
data.description: Delays in the delivery of letters have been causing frustration among residents in the United Kingdom, leading to criticism of the country's longstanding postal service, which dates back 500 years.

data.title: Kevin Warsh, crypto advocate, nominated by Trump to replace Powell as Fed chair. Senate confirmation ahead.
data.description: President Trump has chosen Kevin Warsh, a former Federal Reserve Governor, as his nominee to replace Jerome Powell as the chair of the Federal Reserve. This nomination will lead to a Senate confirmation process that could potentially be contentious. Jerome Powell currently serves as the Federal Reserve chair, and if the nomination is successful, Warsh will take over this critical role. This decision by President Trump will now undergo scrutiny and evaluation by the Senate before Warsh can assume office.

data.title: Chung-Ang University scientists in Seoul generate electricity using a Tesla turbine-inspired structure with practical compressed air.
data.description: A novel method of generating electricity without direct contact has been developed, utilizing compressed air and a Tesla turbine design in Seoul, South Korea. This innovation comes in response to the growing energy needs in multiple industries. Over the past few years, researchers have been investigating the technique, with remarkable findings. The approach aims to enhance energy production efficiency and minimize environmental impact. This development marks a significant step forward in the pursuit of sustainable energy solutions.

data.description: In recent data reported, the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.5%, reflecting a decrease of 0.3% from the previous month. data.title: Autoliv Inc. recovers almost all tariff costs in Q4, with sales of $2.817 billion surpassing estimates. CEO cites mainly strong growth in India and with Chinese OEMs. The firm cuts headcount by 900, posts an adjusted EPS of $3.19.
data.description: Autoliv, Inc. (NYSE: ALV) reported strong fourth-quarter results with adjusted earnings per share of $3.19, surpassing the analyst estimate of $2.90. The company also revealed quarterly sales of $2.817 billion, exceeding Street expectations of $2.772 billion. Organic net sales increased by 4.2%, mainly due to new product launches. Autoliv's CEO, Mikael Bratt, mentioned a record high sales quarter and full year, driven by significant growth in India and with Chinese OEMs. Sales to COEMs grew by almost 40% in the quarter and by 23% for the full year. The company recovered nearly all tariff costs in Q4 and over 80% for the full year. Despite a 3.9% rise in gross profit to $572 million, operating income decreased by 9.6% to $319 million. The operating margin contracted due to margin pressure. Autoliv also reduced its headcount by about 900 year over year.
